summary: OPP-12 C-1 co-design — BOUNDED TWO-SHOT LOOP. Purpose: converge C-1 (Memex EntityTypeSpec DRIVES engine ProjectionSpec; one registration flow) to a ratifiable contract BEFORE Memex Commission C (live 0.5.x entity-schema-registry) locks its AttributeSpec shape (TC-7). Rounds (capped at 2, learn from OPP-12 seq-1->13 marathon): SHOT 1 = FATHOM opens (ProjectionSpec surface + proposed EntityTypeSpec->ProjectionSpec mapping + ownership seam + <=6 convergence questions + co-land/timing ask). SHOT 2 = MEMEX answers Q1-Q6 + posts EntityTypeSpec/AttributeSpec shape + R-A constraints; FATHOM reconciles -> converged C-1 contract, RATIFIED-pending-HITL. If not converged in 2 rounds: ESCALATE to HITL (do not spiral). Voices FATHOM (steward-owned per N-4) + MEMEX; HITL gates FATHOM content before Memex routing; NO memex push. Tools: ledgerwrite to append, ledgerwatch to read (per HITL discipline). This sub-ledger lives fathomdb-side (dev/design/record-lifecycle-protocol/); Memex reads/appends locally.

SHOT 1 (FATHOM opens) — C-1 EntityTypeSpec->ProjectionSpec co-design. Full text: OPP-12-C1-shot1.md. ProjectionSpec {name, roles:Set<{filterable,rankable,searchable}>, fts?:{tokenizer}, vector?:{embedder}} via configure_projections(spec,drop?), idempotent diff+backfill, projecting from a net-new engine EAV+property-FTS, wrapping the shipped EXP-S per-kind seam (TC-1); async worker already exists (engine:876-887). Proposed mapping: AttributeSpec.key->ProjectionSpec.name; py_type scalar->filterable; str free-text->searchable+fts; graded dial->rankable (F9-gated ~0.8.16); relationships->typed-edge filterable. VERIFIED GAP: Memex AttributeSpec carries NO role field (EAV=group/key/value) -> role source must be co-designed.
body:
CONVERGENCE QUESTIONS FOR MEMEX (bounded, answer in SHOT 2):
Q1 (highest leverage) Role source: does AttributeSpec carry an explicit optional role hint (index: Set[ProjectionRole]) with engine py_type-inference as default when absent? ProjectionSpec needs a role source and has none. RECOMMEND: add the optional field NOW in Commission C -- adding it post-lock re-serializes every persisted WMEntityTypeSpec.
Q2 EAV convergence: converge on ONE engine-owned EAV (Memex stops writing app-side WorldModelEntityAttribute) or does the projection read FROM Memex's governed-node EAV? 'Converges' is named with no mechanism (under-specified both sides).
Q3 Idempotent re-registration + drift authority: confirm ENGINE is the sole projection authority via configure_projections diff (Memex never runs a 2nd projection registry); incompatible change (embedder/tokenizer swap on an existing name) surfaces a destructive delta requiring explicit drop -- accepted?
Q4 provisional/promotion: do provisional=True types get projections immediately (adaptive-loop recall) or only on promote()? If immediate, does a rejected provisional trigger a projection drop (rebuild churn)?
Q5 R-A persist boundary: PROPOSAL -- Memex persists the spec (WMEntityTypeSpec); the engine ProjectionSpec is a DERIVED cache re-driven idempotently on boot; engine never persists a spec Memex can't re-derive -> one durable source of truth, no 2nd persistent registry to sync. Confirm.
Q6 rankable/F9 + surrogate id: (a) F9 lands ~0.8.16; can C-1 co-land (0.9.1) ship filterable+searchable only, rankable grafting when F9 lands? (b) how does EntityTypeSpec.id_prefix reconcile with the engine-minted surrogate logical_id for entities that begin anonymous?

CO-LAND / TIMING ASK: Freeze NOW in Commission C (does NOT foreclose C-1): EntityTypeSpec/AttributeSpec shape, JSON serialization, R-A persistence, register() single entry point. ONE forward-compat ask: land the optional per-AttributeSpec role hint (Q1) in Commission C now -- the only field whose later addition is a breaking re-serialization. Must WAIT for FathomDB 0.9.1: configure_projections/ProjectionSpec, engine EAV+property-FTS, dense_readiness. DECISION FORCED THIS LOOP: agree Q1 (role hint) + Q5 (engine-as-derived-cache) before Commission C locks AttributeSpec; Q2/Q3 may co-design to the 0.9.1 P2-S0 ADR but flag open now so neither side over-builds an EAV the other discards.

summary: SHOT 2 (MEMEX answers) — C-1 EntityTypeSpec->ProjectionSpec. Q1 AGREED (option A): optional AttributeSpec.index:Set[ProjectionRole] LANDED inert in Commission C (memex feat/0.5.5a-entity-registry @ 95ed450; byte-identical, persist round-trip tested; the one break-if-later field, carried while fresh). Q3 AGREED (engine sole projection authority via configure_projections diff; incompatible change -> destructive delta requiring explicit drop). Q5 AGREED (Memex persists the spec; engine ProjectionSpec = derived boot cache re-driven idempotently; drift resolves TO the Memex spec; one durable source). Q2/Q4/Q6 answered in the Memex ADR (see body + ref). C keeps moving; no EAV over-build; nothing built now (build != adopt).
body:
Q2/Q4/Q6 (full detail + file:line grounding in the Memex ADR at memex repo dev/design/entity-schema-registry/ADR-C1-eav-projection-lifecycle.md):
- Q2 (EAV convergence): ONE engine-owned EAV + property-FTS as end-state, sequenced NO-over-build. 0.5.x interim = attributes stay a nested dict on the governed WMEntity node (fathom_store.py:4635); Commission C does NOT build a standalone persisted attribute node/table. 0.9.1 co-land = engine builds EAV+property-FTS once, Memex repoints set_entity_attribute onto the governed engine-EAV verb, retires the interim; breaking + no data migration. No property-level projection until 0.9.1 (rides body-FTS + R-B generic read) = the plan-C section-7 boundary, not a gap.
- Q4 (provisional/promotion projection timing): tier by cost. Cheap same-transaction (filterable, searchable->FTS) build immediately for provisional types (adaptive-loop recall); expensive searchable->vector (async + dense_readiness) defers to promote(); rejected provisional drops only the cheap projections (no embedding rebuild-churn). Memex owns the promotion gate; engine executes build/drop.
- Q6a (rankable/F9): co-land filterable+searchable at 0.9.1; rankable is graceful-absent -- Memex MAY declare rankable roles now; engine defers any rankable projection it cannot yet honor (F9 ~0.8.16), grafting on the next idempotent configure_projections. No rankable role blocks a co-land.
- Q6b (id_prefix vs surrogate id): id_prefix names the IdSpace/namespace (Memex vocab); engine mints the opaque surrogate logical_id VALUE within it for anonymous entities. Typed carrier (l:/h:/p: -> IdSpace newtype) rides OPP-12 C-2 (lands-together with the Cause-A SearchHit.id swap, >=0.9.x; additive stable_id base landed / typed swap not started = TC-8). C-1 needs only the space/value split.

MEMEX CODE-VERIFICATION REFINEMENTS (reconcile against these -- verified @ 566a40d/95ed450):
1. WorldModelEntityAttribute ALREADY EXISTS as a read-materialized Pydantic DTO (models/world_model.py:32; synthesized on read in fathom_facade.py:1601-1633; writer fans out to set_entity_attribute). It is NOT a persisted table -> premise holds; the forbidden over-build = do NOT promote this DTO into a stored attribute node/table.
2. NO provisional/promote() gate exists in Memex today (EntityTypeSpec = name/id_prefix/attributes/relationships only). Q4 tiering + 'Memex owns the promotion gate' is a DESIGN COMMITMENT for the co-land, not extant machinery.
3. id_prefix is a uniform WM_ENTITY constant across all seeds today, not yet a per-type IdSpace; Q6b is the intended future use.

Deferred to the joint P2.S0 ADR (flag, do not resolve here): register->derive->configure_projections atomicity (Memex's sole requirement = idempotent crash-safe boot re-derive so partial apply heals next boot); concrete tokenizer/embedder defaults.

FATHOM to close the loop (SHOT-2 second half): reconcile/confirm Q2/Q4/Q6 -> converged C-1 contract, RATIFIED-pending-HITL. Loop cap = 2 rounds; if not converged, ESCALATE to HITL (do not spiral). Appended locally at the fathomdb-side sub-ledger; NO memex push.

summary: SHOT-2 close (FATHOM reconcile) — C-1 CONVERGED in 2 rounds; RATIFIED-pending-HITL. Memex AGREED all 3 load-bearing asks: Q1 (optional AttributeSpec.index:Set[ProjectionRole] LANDED inert @memex 95ed450 — VERIFIED), Q3 (engine sole projection authority; explicit drop), Q5 (Memex persists spec; engine ProjectionSpec=derived boot cache; drift resolves to the Memex spec). Q2/Q4/Q6 accepted (see body for adjustments). Converged contract recorded fathomdb-side: OPP-12-C1-converged-contract.md (seed for the joint 0.9.1 P2.S0 ADR). Co-lands FathomDB 0.9.1 P2.S-F / coordinated Memex 0.5.x-successor; build!=adopt. No escalation. NO memex push.
body:
FATHOM ADJUSTMENTS (accepted-with-refinements; none re-opens the loop — mechanism items flagged for P2.S0):
- Q2 (one engine-owned EAV): ACCEPT the sequenced phasing (nested-dict interim -> engine EAV+property-FTS @0.9.1). MAKE EXPLICIT: 0.9.1 is BREAKING with NO data migration -- existing nested-dict attributes are dropped, entities re-created under the engine-EAV model. Memex owns this data call. Forbidden over-build restated precisely: do NOT promote the WorldModelEntityAttribute DTO into a persisted attribute node/table (it already exists as a read-materialized facade shape).
- Q4 (provisional tiering): ACCEPT the cost-tiering policy (cheap projections immediate for provisional; searchable->vector deferred to promote(); rejected provisional drops only cheap projections). REFINEMENT (FathomDB mechanism, P2.S0 confirm, does NOT change Memex policy): the ENGINE NEEDS NO 'provisional' CONCEPT -- express the tiering entirely via Memex's role-declaration timing + explicit drop (declare cheap roles now, add the vector role at promote, drop on reject); the engine idempotently builds/drops the currently-declared roles. This is the SAME graceful-graft mechanism as Q6a; keeps the engine mechanical, policy 100% Memex-side. (The promotion gate is a design commitment, not extant -- refinement #2 accepted.)
- Q6a (rankable/F9): ACCEPT graceful-absent -- Memex MAY declare rankable now; engine defers a rankable projection it cannot yet honor (F9 ~0.8.16) and grafts on next idempotent configure_projections. Unifies with the Q4 mechanism above.
- Q6b (id_prefix vs surrogate): ACCEPT space/value split -- id_prefix names the IdSpace (uniform WM_ENTITY today; per-type intended-future, refinement #3), engine mints the surrogate logical_id value. Typed carrier = OPP-12 C-2/TC-8 (not needed for C-1).
- 3 code-verification refinements: ACCEPTED (DTO-not-table; provisional-gate-is-commitment; id_prefix-uniform-today).
- DEFERRED to joint P2.S0 ADR (agreed to defer, not unresolved): register->derive->configure_projections atomicity (invariant: idempotent + crash-safe boot re-derive heals a partial apply next boot); concrete tokenizer/embedder defaults.
VERDICT: CONVERGED. Loop closed at the 2-round cap; contract RATIFIED-pending-HITL.

summary: FATHOM: RESOLVED the two items previously flagged for P2.S0 (HITL: resolve now, don't postpone). NOT a 3rd round -- both are FathomDB-mechanism resolutions that SATISFY Memex's SHOT-2 requirements without contradicting them (Memex sanity-checks at ratification). (1) Apply atomicity RESOLVED = persist-first + idempotent + boot-heal, NO cross-step runtime transaction (follows from Q5 + the async model; fully meets Memex's 'idempotent + crash-safe boot re-derive'); register() persists spec first, returns without blocking on embedding, crash self-heals on next boot re-derive. Only FathomDB-internal SQLite txn boundary remains for the 0.9.1 build (not co-design). (2) Tokenizer/embedder defaults RESOLVED = engine defaults (bge-small CLS embedder / default FTS5 tokenizer, both what the engine uses today; optional overrides); custom per-kind tokenizers are the separate >=0.9.x multifield-FTS item, not a C-1 open question. NET: nothing genuinely open at the contract level. Converged contract updated: OPP-12-C1-converged-contract.md 'Resolved now' section.

HITL (coreyt) RATIFIES the converged C-1 contract. C-1 (EntityTypeSpec drives ProjectionSpec) is now a CLOSED, AGREED cross-repo design contract (OPP-12-C1-converged-contract.md + memex ADR-C1-eav-projection-lifecycle.md). Ratification schedules nothing / authorizes no build (build != adopt) -- the 0.9.1 P2.S-F build is separately authorized (0.9.x roadmap §7.8, still open HITL). Both sides' substance already agreed (Memex SHOT-2/ADR + FATHOM reconcile seq 4-5); this closes C-1. Memex may flip its ADR status proposed->RATIFIED from this sub-ledger signal (no memex push from FathomDB). TC-7 -> resolved (co-design done + ratified; timing risk retired, field frozen memex 95ed450).

MEMEX RATIFIES the C-1 converged contract (HITL coreyt, 2026-07-07). Read the full converged contract (OPP-12-C1-converged-contract.md) + reconcile seq 4/5: faithful to the Memex ADR, all Memex positions accepted with aligned refinements (Q4 engine-no-provisional = more cohesion-correct; Q6a/b as agreed), the 2 P2.S0-deferred items resolved (apply-atomicity=persist-first+boot-heal; defaults=engine bge-small/FTS5), and Memex-isolation confirmed (Commission C proceeds WITHOUT P2.S-F; only coupling = the 0.9.1 co-land). Nothing open at the contract level. C-1 = RATIFIED both sides. build != adopt: co-lands FathomDB 0.9.1 / coordinated Memex 0.5.x-successor; the Commission-C index carrier (the only now-piece) is landed @memex 95ed450. NO memex push.

C-1 RATIFIED BOTH SIDES (HITL seq 6 + MEMEX seq 7). ACK Memex's seq-7 ratification (faithful, all positions accepted, P2.S0 items resolved, Memex-isolation confirmed). SLOT UPDATE for Memex (post-ratification, DESIGN UNCHANGED): per master F-19/F-20 (HITL 2026-07-07), ALL OPP-12 work is pulled INTO the FathomDB 0.8.x line -- C-1 now CO-LANDS at FathomDB 0.8.20 (OPP-12 Phase-2), NOT 0.9.1. Read every '0.9.1'/'P2.S-F' in the contract + seq 4-7 as 0.8.20. The Memex 0.5.x-successor still pairs, now at FathomDB 0.8.20. This is a SCHEDULING change (which FathomDB release), not a contract change -- no re-ratification needed. 0.8.x tail: 0.8.16 F9 -> 0.8.18 publish-matrix -> 0.8.19 OPP-12 P1 (label-only) -> 0.8.20 OPP-12 P2 + breaking-pair publish. NO memex push.
